Title: Variations in carbon flux allocation among cassava (Manihot esculenta) cultivars arise from balanced competition between starch accumulation and structural component development

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher hexokinase activity in FX01 is associated with more efficient glucose/fructose phosphorylation, higher starch precursor flux, and greater starch accumulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HXK activity was significantly higher in FX01 than SC16. 2. The paper concludes that efficient glucose and fructose phosphorylation is a key determinant of starch metabolic flux. 3. Enhanced hexose phosphorylation in FX01 coincided with higher F6P/G6P/ADP-glucose enrichment and starch accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme activity assay; 13C pulse-labeling metabolite/isotopomer analysis; starch accumulation analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured as enzyme activity in storage roots
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower HXK-associated phosphorylation capacity correlates with reduced starch synthesis and greater diversion to structural components.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Improving hexose phosphorylation capacity is highlighted as a candidate route for high-starch breeding in cassava.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Measured as enzyme activity; no specific cassava HXK isoform was identified in the paper.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher sucrose synthase activity in FX01 supports efficient sucrose catabolism and starch accumulation; lower activity in SC16 contributes to sucrose backlogging.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SuSy activity was significantly higher in FX01 than SC16 roots. 2. The authors conclude that post-unloading sucrose metabolism efficiency, rather than sucrose availability, underlies divergent starch accumulation. 3. Lower SuSy activity in SC16 is linked to metabolic bottleneck and reduced starch flux.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme activity assay; soluble sugar/sucrose measurements; 13C pulse-labeling metabolite flux analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured as enzyme activity in storage roots
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower activity associated with futile sucrose cycling and reduced starch accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate metabolic target to improve sucrose utilization efficiency for starch accumulation in storage roots.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as the primary route for sucrose metabolism in cassava symplastic unloading pathway.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CWI activity in SC16 reflects compensatory apoplastic sucrose hydrolysis associated with metabolic backlogging rather than efficient starch accumulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CWI activity was significantly higher in SC16 than FX01. 2. Authors interpret this as a minor, compensatory response to inefficient intracellular sucrose processing in SC16. 3. Elevated CWI did not rescue starch accumulation in the low-starch cultivar.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme activity assay; soluble sugar/sucrose measurements; physiological interpretation with 13C labeling data
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured as enzyme activity in storage roots
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher CWI activity is linked to futile sucrose turnover and energetic burden in SC16.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Cautionary marker for inefficient carbon partitioning; not presented as a direct positive breeding target.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states CWI likely plays a minor role in cassava sucrose metabolism because cassava predominantly unloads symplastically.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher SPS activity under saturating substrate conditions in SC16 suggests higher sucrose synthesis capacity and contribution to futile sucrose cycling rather than starch gain.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. No difference in SPS activity under limiting substrate conditions between cultivars. 2. SC16 had significantly higher SPS activity under saturating conditions. 3. Together with high CWI, this suggests active futile cycling of sucrose synthesis and degradation in SC16, imposing energetic cost and reducing starch accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme activity assay under limiting and saturating substrate conditions; integration with sugar and flux analyses
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured as enzyme activity in storage roots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate-limiting versus saturating assay conditions; 13CO2 labeling study context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with futile sucrose cycling and reduced carbon-use efficiency for starch accumulation in SC16.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful physiological indicator of inefficient carbon allocation; should be balanced with sucrose utilization traits in breeding.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ferulic-acid-derived lignin biosynthesis and lignin accumulation; silencing reduces lignin and increases ADP-glucose and starch in storage roots.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeCOMT8 transcript abundance was higher in low-starch, high-lignin SC16 storage roots than in FX01. 2. Recombinant MeCOMT8 showed catalytic activity against caffeic acid in vitro, supporting its role in ferulic acid formation. 3. VIGS silencing of MeCOMT8 reduced lignin autofluorescence, lignin content, coniferyl alcohol and sinapyl alcohol, while increasing ADP-glucose and starch content in cassava storage roots.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; 13C pulse-labeling metabolite flux analysis; recombinant protein enzyme assay and kinetics in vitro; virus-induced gene silencing (VIGS); lignin autofluorescence microscopy; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; starch and lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates caffeic acid to form ferulic acid using S-adenosyl-L-methionine as methyl donor.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: monolignol biosynthesis; caffeic acid-to-ferulic acid step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies immediate precursor pool for monolignol synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a critical/rate-limiting step for lignin flux in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in storage roots of SC16 than FX01 during monitored post-labeling stages
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 13CO2 pulse labeling; greenhouse-grown plants; VIGS in storage roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Non-targeted metabolomics; 13C metabolite flux analysis; raw metabolomics deposited in MetaboLights: MTBLS13541
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher MeCOMT8 activity is associated with greater lignin deposition and lower starch accumulation, indicating reduced storage quality/high-starch trait.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong target for high-starch cassava improvement: downregulation/editing of MeCOMT8 may reduce lignin and redirect carbon to starch, but potential effects on structural integrity/vascular development should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note possible VIGS off-target limitation, but combined flux, enzyme kinetics, and silencing data support MeCOMT8 as pivotal in carbon partitioning between starch and lignin.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon storage and food/industrial starch reserve
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with enhanced AGPase activity and greater starch accumulation in high-starch cultivar FX01.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeAGPase1.3 expression was significantly higher in FX01 storage roots than in SC16. 2. Higher AGPase activity in FX01 coincided with increased ADP-glucose and faster starch accumulation. 3. The study identifies glucose/fructose phosphorylation and AGPase-supported precursor conversion as major determinants of high starch flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; AGPase enzyme activity assay; 13C pulse-labeling metabolite profiling; starch accumulation measurements
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts glucose-1-phosphate and ATP into ADP-glucose, the activated glucosyl donor for starch synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ADP-glucose formation step in amyloplast starch pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key driver/control step for starch accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in FX01 storage roots during monitored stages
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 13CO2 pulse labeling during storage root bulking
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Non-targeted metabolomics and 13C metabolite flux analysis; MetaboLights MTBLS13541
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Carbon allocation toward starch may reduce allocation to structural components such as lignin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for increasing cassava starch content, potentially in combination with improved hexose phosphorylation and reduced lignin-flux genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper links findings to prior evidence that MeAPL3/AGPase activity controls starch content variation across cassava genotypes.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in FX01 is associated with enhanced starch biosynthesis/accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeGBSS-1 transcripts were higher in FX01 than SC16. 2. Elevated expression aligned with stronger starch synthesis phenotype in FX01. 3. The gene is part of the starch-biosynthetic expression program distinguishing high- versus low-starch cultivars.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; starch content/accumulation analysis; 13C metabolite flux context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates glucan chains using ADP-glucose during starch biosynthesis, especially amylose synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate component for boosting starch biosynthesis in high-starch breeding stacks.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct gene manipulation performed in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in FX01 is associated with stronger starch accumulation in storage roots.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeSSIII expression was significantly higher in FX01 than SC16. 2. This higher expression accompanied increased AGPase activity and starch accumulation in the high-starch cultivar. 3. MeSSIII is part of the starch-synthesis gene set associated with superior starch flux in FX01.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; starch accumulation measurements; 13C metabolite flux context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate α-1,4-glucan chains during starch biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for combinatorial engineering of cassava starch production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct manipulation in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall strengthening and vascular support
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression/activity context in SC16 is associated with faster lignin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeCAD15 expression was significantly higher in SC16 than FX01. 2. CAD enzyme activity was consistently higher in SC16 across time points. 3. This corresponded to faster lignin accumulation in the low-starch cultivar.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; CAD enzyme activity assay; 13C flux analysis of lignin intermediates; lignin accumulation measurements
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamaldehydes to monolignol alcohols such as coniferyl alcohol and sinapyl alcohol.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursors of lignin polymer
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher lignin deposition associates with reduced starch accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to lower lignification and improve starch content, though likely secondary to MeCOMT8 in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene-level causality was not directly tested by silencing/overexpression in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in SC16 is associated with greater ferulic acid/lignin flux, but direct causal validation was not performed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeCOMT1 expression was significantly upregulated in SC16 compared with FX01. 2. Its expression pattern paralleled increased ferulic acid accumulation and lignin deposition in SC16. 3. The paper discusses COMT1/8 together, but functional validation focused on MeCOMT8.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely methylates caffeic acid-related substrates toward ferulate/monolignol pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not directly established for this isoform
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Association with increased lignin and reduced starch allocation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible additional target for reducing lignin flux, but less validated than MeCOMT8.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a focal measured lignin-pathway gene in Results, though not the principal validated enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall strengthening and vascular support
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in SC16 is associated with enhanced lignin biosynthesis context.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeCCR2 expression was significantly higher in SC16 than FX01. 2. Upregulation coincided with faster lignin accumulation in SC16. 3. The study uses it as a key lignin-pathway marker accompanying flux divergence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; 13C flux analysis correlation; lignin accumulation measurements
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters to corresponding aldehydes in monolignol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: coniferyl aldehyde and related monolignol aldehydes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor pool for lignin monomers
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Association with increased structural carbon allocation and reduced starch.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate secondary target for modifying lignification in cassava roots.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct perturbation of this gene in the study.
